title: Shivaji's Quiet Escape from Agra
subtitle: Agra Fort to the Deccan, 1666 CE
moral: Courage is sometimes the calm intelligence to wait, plan, and act at the right moment.

sources.citation: Krishnaji Anant Sabhasad, Sabhasad Bakhar, tr. S.N. Sen as Siva Chhatrapati
sources.note: Early Maratha narrative for Shivaji's life, Agra episode, and Swaraj memory.

sources.citation: Jedhe Shakavali, entries for Shivaji's Agra visit and return
sources.note: Maratha chronological record supporting dates and sequence.

sources.citation: Jadunath Sarkar, Shivaji and His Times, Agra chapters
sources.note: Critical comparison of Maratha and Persian sources.

dialogue.text: Shivaji is kept under watch after a tense visit.

dialogue.speaker: chhatrapati_shivaji_maharaj
dialogue.text: Chains can’t hold a quiet mind.
He waits, watching every routine, every guard’s step.

dialogue.speaker: chhatrapati_shivaji_maharaj
dialogue.text: No haste. The right moment will come.
Tradition remembers secret plans and hidden baskets.

dialogue.speaker: chhatrapati_shivaji_maharaj
dialogue.text: Let the world think I sleep. My mind travels home.

panels.shot: bird's-eye of fort courtyard at dusk

dialogue.text: After weeks of patience, the path is quietly tested.

dialogue.speaker: chhatrapati_shivaji_maharaj
dialogue.text: Step small, heart steady. No one must suspect.

panels.shot: wide night shot on road leaving Agra

dialogue.text: Beyond Agra, he slips into the night roads.

dialogue.speaker: chhatrapati_shivaji_maharaj
dialogue.text: The Deccan waits. Our people must know I live.

panels.shot: wide shot of Deccan hill-fort at sunrise

dialogue.text: From Agra’s walls to Deccan’s hills, patience carried him home.

dialogue.speaker: chhatrapati_shivaji_maharaj
dialogue.text: We fight not only with swords, but with steady minds.
Courage is sometimes the calm intelligence to wait, plan, and act at the right moment.
